title: VTEP Reachability and Loss
spl:
index=network sourcetype="vtep:probe" earliest=-24h
| where loss_pct>2 OR latency_ms>10
| stats avg(loss_pct) as avg_loss, avg(latency_ms) as avg_lat by src_vtep, dst_vtep
| sort -avg_loss
| head 20
description: Packet loss and latency between VTEP peers ‚Äî augments UC-18.3.9 state-only checks.
value: Packet loss and latency between VTEP peers ‚Äî augments UC-18.3.9 state-only checks.
implementation: Run periodic probes between TEP IPs from automation. Correlate with underlay QoS drops.
visualization: Heatmap (VTEP √ó VTEP loss), Table (worst pairs), Line chart (loss trend).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_nix`, `TA-cisco_ios`, `arista:eos` via SC4S, ICMP probes, SNMP.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `vtep:probe` or synthetic tests.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Run periodic probes between TEP IPs from automation. Correlate with underlay QoS drops.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=network sourcetype="vtep:probe" earliest=-24h
| where loss_pct>2 OR latency_ms>10
| stats avg(loss_pct) as avg_loss, avg(latency_ms) as avg_lat by src_vtep, dst_vtep
| sort -avg_loss
| head 20
```

Understanding this SPL

**VTEP Reachability and Loss** ‚Äî Packet loss and latency between VTEP peers ‚Äî augments UC-18.3.9 state-only checks.

Documented **Data sources**: `vtep:probe` or synthetic tests. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_nix`, `TA-cisco_ios`, `arista:eos` via SC4S, ICMP probes, SNMP. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: network; **sourcetype**: vtep:probe. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=network, sourcetype="vtep:probe", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where loss_pct>2 OR latency_ms>10` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by src_vtep, dst_vtep** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
‚Ä¢ Limits the number of rows with `head`.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Heatmap (VTEP √ó VTEP loss), Table (worst pairs), Line chart (loss trend).
grandmaExplanation: Packet loss and latency between VTEP peers ‚Äî augments UC-18.3.9 state-only checks ‚Äî so traffic problems, routing mistakes, and risky changes surface fast.
